# UC-14.1.1 · HVAC Performance Monitoring

## Description

HVAC issues in data centers risk equipment damage; in buildings they affect occupant comfort and energy costs.

## Value

HVAC issues in data centers risk equipment damage; in buildings they affect occupant comfort and energy costs.

## Implementation

Connect BMS to Splunk via MQTT broker or Modbus gateway. Ingest setpoints and actuals per zone. Alert when deviation exceeds 3°F/2°C for sustained period. Track energy consumption per HVAC unit.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Modbus TA, MQTT input, BMS API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: BACnet/Modbus sensors (temperature setpoint, actual, supply/return air).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Connect BMS to Splunk via MQTT broker or Modbus gateway. Ingest setpoints and actuals per zone. Alert when deviation exceeds 3°F/2°C for sustained period. Track energy consumption per HVAC unit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=bms sourcetype="modbus:hvac"
| eval deviation=abs(actual_temp-setpoint_temp)
| where deviation > 3
| table _time, zone, setpoint_temp, actual_temp, deviation
```

Understanding this SPL

**HVAC Performance Monitoring** — HVAC issues in data centers risk equipment damage; in buildings they affect occupant comfort and energy costs.

Documented **Data sources**: BACnet/Modbus sensors (temperature setpoint, actual, supply/return air). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Modbus TA, MQTT input, BMS API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: bms; **sourcetype**: modbus:hvac. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=bms, sourcetype="modbus:hvac". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **deviation** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where deviation > 3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **HVAC Performance Monitoring**): table _time, zone, setpoint_temp, actual_temp, deviation

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search in a known window. Spot-check a few events against the BMS console (for example Niagara, Tridium, or Honeywell) and confirm zones, values, and alarms line up. Verify field names and that the right roles can search the index.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (setpoint vs actual per zone), Heatmap (zone × temperature), Single value (zones out of spec).


## SPL

```spl
index=bms sourcetype="modbus:hvac"
| eval deviation=abs(actual_temp-setpoint_temp)
| where deviation > 3
| table _time, zone, setpoint_temp, actual_temp, deviation
```

## Visualization

Line chart (setpoint vs actual per zone), Heatmap (zone × temperature), Single value (zones out of spec).
